specs.section_path: 15-Layer Summary Table > 3.2 The Dynamic Spine: The Unified Phase Dynamics Equation (UPDE) > The Unified Phase Dynamics Equation (UPDE) > Meta-Framework Integrations
source_formulae: P0R02605: Phase Slips as Prediction Error:
P0R02606: A "phase slip," or a deviation from the expected coherence, is the physical instantiation of prediction error.

P0R02607: Inter-Layer Coupling as Inference: The C_InterLayer term is the mechanism of hierarchical inference. The downward coupling (F_D) is the propagation of top-down predictions from a higher, slower layer to a lower, faster layer. The upward coupling (G_U) is the propagation of bottom-up prediction error from the lower layer to the higher one, compelling the higher layer to update its model. The entire UPDE describes a network that is continuously and dynamically working to minimise its prediction error (phase desynchronisation) at every level of the hierarchy.
test_protocols: preserve Phase Slips as Prediction Error: source-accounting boundary
